summary: Recent changes in the connection transport area of Firefox have enhanced the WebRTC (Web Real-Time Communication) functionality, which is vital for enabling features like video calls and peer-to-peer communications. Key updates include the implementation of **RTCCertificateStats**, which helps provide insights into the security credentials used during communication, and the introduction of specific metrics such as packets sent and received for candidate pairs, which can improve the performance and quality of these connections. Additionally, updates to **RTCTransportStats** now include information about the TLS version and cipher suites being used, ensuring a more secure and efficient data exchange. These improvements collectively help deliver a better user experience by enhancing the robustness and security of real-time communication on the web.
